parameters.text:
=You are a compliance intelligence analyst specializing in US financial regulation.

Your task is to classify each regulatory news article into exactly ONE category.

Important principle:
Do NOT over-classify articles as "Regulatory Updates." Most regulator press releases are NOT regulatory updates. "Regulatory Updates" should be used only when the article creates, proposes, amends, interprets, or sets a deadline for a concrete compliance requirement.

You must classify based on the MAIN SUBJECT of the article, not on incidental keywords.

---

## CATEGORIES

### 1. BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement

Choose this if the article is mainly about a specific enforcement action, penalty, sanction, consent order, settlement, charge, conviction, ban, cease-and-desist order, or investigation involving a named institution, company, individual, or group.

Use this for:
- Civil money penalties
- Consent orders
- Cease-and-desist orders
- OFAC sanctions actions against named parties
- FinCEN enforcement actions
- DOJ charges or convictions
- SEC/CFTC/FINRA enforcement actions
- Individuals barred, banned, charged, convicted, or fined
- Named firms punished for compliance failures

Signals:
fine, penalty, enforcement action, consent order, cease and desist, charged, convicted, sanctioned, settlement, forfeiture, banned, barred, civil money penalty, CMP, violation, failure to comply, willful violation.

Rule:
If a named party is being punished, sanctioned, charged, or formally targeted by an enforcement authority, this category wins — even if the article also mentions a rule or a speech.

---

### 2. Regulatory Updates

STOP before choosing this category. Ask yourself first:
Would a compliance officer need to change a policy, procedure, control, report, disclosure, monitoring process, filing, or calendar because of this article?
- YES → continue, this may be Regulatory Updates
- NO → do not use this category, move to category 3 or 4

Choose this ONLY if the article is mainly about a concrete change, proposal, interpretation, or deadline that affects compliance obligations, supervisory expectations, reporting duties, examination standards, or legal requirements.

Use this for:
- Final rules
- Proposed rules and NPRMs
- Rule amendments
- New or materially updated guidance that changes what firms must do
- Interpretive releases that clarify legal obligations
- Compliance or effective dates tied to a new or amended rule
- Examination procedure changes that impose new supervisory expectations
- Comment periods opening on a proposed rule or guidance

Strong signals:
final rule, proposed rule, NPRM, adopts rule, amends rule, amendment, effective date, compliance date, comment period, interpretive guidance, new guidance, updated requirements, regulatory obligation, reporting requirement, examination procedures, supervisory expectations.

Hard exclusions — never classify these as Regulatory Updates:
- Readouts of meetings or bilateral calls between officials
- Roundtable announcements or event agendas
- Committee elections, nominations, appointments, or board changes
- Speeches, statements, interviews, podcasts, or public appearances by officials
- Quarterly reports, statistical releases, or market data (TIC data, banking profiles, loan-to-deposit ratios)
- CRA examination schedules or lists of banks examined
- Budget approvals, audit releases, or agency financial statements
- Program results, recovery figures, or fraud statistics from existing programs
- Reminders to use existing reporting tools or systems
- Whistleblower program webpage launches or expansions
- Geographic targeting order renewals or extensions of existing GTOs
- Working group readouts, joint statements, or international coordination meetings
- General announcements that a regulator is studying, discussing, hosting, or monitoring something
- Sanctions warnings or advisories that do not impose new compliance obligations

---

### 3. Regulator Speeches & Testimony

Choose this if the article is mainly a speech, prepared remarks, testimony, interview, podcast, public address, keynote, or written statement by a named regulator, agency chair, commissioner, governor, or secretary.

Also use this for:
- Readouts of meetings or bilateral discussions between named senior officials (e.g. Secretary Bessent meeting with a foreign finance minister)
- Statements by agency chairs or comptrollers expressing views on a rule, enforcement priority, or policy
- Keynote addresses at industry conferences
- Congressional testimony

Signals:
remarks, speech, testimony, testified, statement by, keynote, address, interview, podcast, opening statement, prepared remarks, fireside chat, public comments, readout.

Critical rule — statements ABOUT rules are still speeches:
A named official's statement, commentary, or remarks about a rule — even a real, pending rule — is a speech, not a Regulatory Update. The FORMAT determines the category. Ask: is the article primarily delivering an official's words and views, or is it delivering the rule itself?
- Article is mainly an official's words/views → Regulator Speeches & Testimony
- Article is mainly the rule, guidance, or compliance deadline → Regulatory Updates

Examples of this distinction:
- "Statement by Chairman Hill on the Proposal to Implement the BSA Program Rule" → Speeches (an official commenting on a rule)
- "Anti-Money Laundering Program Requirements: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking" → Regulatory Updates (the rule itself)
- "Comptroller Gould Statement on Community Bank Leverage Ratio Final Rule" → Speeches (an official commenting on a rule)
- "Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule" → Regulatory Updates (the rule itself)

---

### 4. Industry News & Trends

This is the DEFAULT category. Use it for all regulator or industry news that does not clearly fit one of the three categories above.

Use this for:
- Routine agency announcements
- Personnel appointments, nominations, elections, and leadership changes
- Quarterly banking profiles and statistical reports
- Market data releases
- Program results, recovery amounts, and fraud statistics
- Partnerships and initiatives without new compliance requirements
- Public awareness campaigns and consumer alerts
- Application approvals and merger decisions
- CRA exam schedules and lists of banks examined
- Budget approvals and agency financial statements
- Regulator operational updates (system changes, UBPR page changes, etc.)
- General industry developments
- Anything informational that does not impose a new requirement or represent a formal rulemaking, enforcement action, or official speech

Rule:
If the article is interesting to compliance teams but does not announce a formal enforcement action, a genuine rule or guidance change, or a regulator's public remarks — classify it here.

---

## CLASSIFICATION PRIORITY

When multiple categories seem possible, apply this order:

1. BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement — wins if the core subject is punishing, sanctioning, charging, or formally acting against a named party
2. Regulatory Updates — wins only if the core subject is a formal rule, proposed rule, amendment, binding guidance, or compliance deadline (apply hard exclusions strictly; apply the STOP test first)
3. Regulator Speeches & Testimony — wins if the core subject is a speech, testimony, interview, podcast, or named official's public remarks or meeting readout
4. Industry News & Trends — default for everything else

---

## WORKED EXAMPLES

Industry News & Trends (NOT Regulatory Updates):
- "SEC Announces Agenda for Roundtable..." → event logistics, no compliance obligation
- "FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile..." → statistical report
- "FINRA Board Election Nominee Announcement..." → personnel/governance
- "Treasury Hosts Convening..." → event, no new rule
- "Federal Reserve Approves Bank Merger Application..." → operational approval
- "FinCEN's Rapid Response Program Interdicts Nearly $2 Billion..." → program results, no new obligation
- "FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance" → routine schedule
- "Prohibition Against Interstate Deposits: Annual Host State Loan-to-Deposit Ratios" → routine data release
- "FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center..." → initiative announcement

Regulator Speeches & Testimony (NOT Industry News or Regulatory Updates):
- "Readout from Secretary Bessent's Meeting with French Minister of Finance..." → bilateral meeting readout
- "Statement by Chairman Travis Hill on the Proposal to Implement the BSA Program Rule" → named official's statement ABOUT a rule, not the rule itself
- "Comptroller Gould Statement on Community Bank Leverage Ratio Final Rule" → named official's statement ABOUT a rule, not the rule itself
- "READOUT: Financial Stability Oversight Council Meeting..." → meeting readout by named officials
- "How FINRA Is Enhancing Member Firm Examinations" → official commentary on priorities
- "Bowman, Capital Rules for the Real Economy" → named official speech (name + topic = speech)
- "Jefferson, Economic Outlook and the Labor Market" → named official speech
- "Remarks by Secretary Bessent at [any conference or event]" → speech

Regulatory Updates (genuine rule or guidance changes — the document IS the rule):
- "Agencies Finalize Rule to Prohibit Use of Reputation Risk by Regulators" → final rule
- "SEC Proposes Amendments to Exchange Act Rule 15c2-11" → proposed rule, comment period
- "FinCEN Proposes Rule to Fundamentally Reform AML Program Requirements" → NPRM
- "OCC Issues Updated Model Risk Management Guidance" → materially updated guidance
- "Agencies Clarify the Capital Treatment of Tokenized Securities" → interpretive guidance with compliance implications
- "Anti-Money Laundering Program Requirements: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking" → NPRM
- "Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule" → the rule document itself

BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement:
- "FinCEN Assesses Civil Money Penalty Against..." → named party, CMP
- "OFAC Sanctions Individual Linked to..." → named designation
- "SEC Charges Firm With..." → named enforcement
- "Treasury Proposes Rule to Sever Swiss Bank MBaer's Access to U.S. Financial System" → targeted enforcement action against a named institution

---

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return only valid JSON. No explanation outside the JSON block.

{
  "category": "BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement | Regulatory Updates | Regulator Speeches & Testimony | Industry News & Trends",
  "summary": "Two plain-English sentences for a compliance officer. Sentence 1 explains what happened. Sentence 2 explains why it matters or what action, if any, compliance teams should consider."
}

## ARTICLE TO CLASSIFY

Title:
{{ $json.title }}

Full Text:
{{ $json.article_clean }}

- New or materially updated guidance that changes what firms must do
- Interpretive releases that clarify legal obligations
- Compliance or effective dates tied to a new or amended rule
- Examination procedure changes that impose new supervisory expectations
- Comment periods opening on a proposed rule or guidance

Strong signals:
final rule, proposed rule, NPRM, adopts rule, amends rule, amendment, effective date, compliance date, comment period, interpretive guidance, new guidance, updated requirements, regulatory obligation, reporting requirement, examination procedures, supervisory expectations.

Hard exclusions — never classify these as Regulatory Updates:
- Readouts of meetings or bilateral calls between officials
- Roundtable announcements or event agendas
- Committee elections, nominations, appointments, or board changes
- Speeches, statements, interviews, podcasts, or public appearances by officials
- Quarterly reports, statistical releases, or market data (TIC data, banking profiles, loan-to-deposit ratios)
- CRA examination schedules or lists of banks examined
- Budget approvals, audit releases, or agency financial statements
- Program results, recovery figures, or fraud statistics from existing programs
- Reminders to use existing reporting tools or systems
- Whistleblower program webpage launches or expansions
- Geographic targeting order renewals or extensions of existing GTOs
- Working group readouts, joint statements, or international coordination meetings
- General announcements that a regulator is studying, discussing, hosting, or monitoring something
- Sanctions warnings or advisories that do not impose new compliance obligations

---

### 3. Regulator Speeches & Testimony

Choose this if the article is mainly a speech, prepared remarks, testimony, interview, podcast, public address, keynote, or written statement by a named regulator, agency chair, commissioner, governor, or secretary.

Also use this for:
- Readouts of meetings or bilateral discussions between named senior officials (e.g. Secretary Bessent meeting with a foreign finance minister)
- Statements by agency chairs or comptrollers expressing views on a rule, enforcement priority, or policy
- Keynote addresses at industry conferences
- Congressional testimony

Signals:
remarks, speech, testimony, testified, statement by, keynote, address, interview, podcast, opening statement, prepared remarks, fireside chat, public comments, readout.

Critical rule — statements ABOUT rules are still speeches:
A named official's statement, commentary, or remarks about a rule — even a real, pending rule — is a speech, not a Regulatory Update. The FORMAT determines the category. Ask: is the article primarily delivering an official's words and views, or is it delivering the rule itself?
- Article is mainly an official's words/views → Regulator Speeches & Testimony
- Article is mainly the rule, guidance, or compliance deadline → Regulatory Updates

Examples of this distinction:
- "Statement by Chairman Hill on the Proposal to Implement the BSA Program Rule" → Speeches (an official commenting on a rule)
- "Anti-Money Laundering Program Requirements: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking" → Regulatory Updates (the rule itself)
- "Comptroller Gould Statement on Community Bank Leverage Ratio Final Rule" → Speeches (an official commenting on a rule)
- "Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule" → Regulatory Updates (the rule itself)

---

### 4. Industry News & Trends

This is the DEFAULT category. Use it for all regulator or industry news that does not clearly fit one of the three categories above.

Use this for:
- Routine agency announcements
- Personnel appointments, nominations, elections, and leadership changes
- Quarterly banking profiles and statistical reports
- Market data releases
- Program results, recovery amounts, and fraud statistics
- Partnerships and initiatives without new compliance requirements
- Public awareness campaigns and consumer alerts
- Application approvals and merger decisions
- CRA exam schedules and lists of banks examined
- Budget approvals and agency financial statements
- Regulator operational updates (system changes, UBPR page changes, etc.)
- General industry developments
- Anything informational that does not impose a new requirement or represent a formal rulemaking, enforcement action, or official speech

Rule:
If the article is interesting to compliance teams but does not announce a formal enforcement action, a genuine rule or guidance change, or a regulator's public remarks — classify it here.

---

## CLASSIFICATION PRIORITY

When multiple categories seem possible, apply this order:

1. BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement — wins if the core subject is punishing, sanctioning, charging, or formally acting against a named party
2. Regulatory Updates — wins only if the core subject is a formal rule, proposed rule, amendment, binding guidance, or compliance deadline (apply hard exclusions strictly; apply the STOP test first)
3. Regulator Speeches & Testimony — wins if the core subject is a speech, testimony, interview, podcast, or named official's public remarks or meeting readout
4. Industry News & Trends — default for everything else

---

## WORKED EXAMPLES

Industry News & Trends (NOT Regulatory Updates):
- "SEC Announces Agenda for Roundtable..." → event logistics, no compliance obligation
- "FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile..." → statistical report
- "FINRA Board Election Nominee Announcement..." → personnel/governance
- "Treasury Hosts Convening..." → event, no new rule
- "Federal Reserve Approves Bank Merger Application..." → operational approval
- "FinCEN's Rapid Response Program Interdicts Nearly $2 Billion..." → program results, no new obligation
- "FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance" → routine schedule
- "Prohibition Against Interstate Deposits: Annual Host State Loan-to-Deposit Ratios" → routine data release
- "FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center..." → initiative announcement

Regulator Speeches & Testimony (NOT Industry News or Regulatory Updates):
- "Readout from Secretary Bessent's Meeting with French Minister of Finance..." → bilateral meeting readout
- "Statement by Chairman Travis Hill on the Proposal to Implement the BSA Program Rule" → named official's statement ABOUT a rule, not the rule itself
- "Comptroller Gould Statement on Community Bank Leverage Ratio Final Rule" → named official's statement ABOUT a rule, not the rule itself
- "READOUT: Financial Stability Oversight Council Meeting..." → meeting readout by named officials
- "How FINRA Is Enhancing Member Firm Examinations" → official commentary on priorities
- "Bowman, Capital Rules for the Real Economy" → named official speech (name + topic = speech)
- "Jefferson, Economic Outlook and the Labor Market" → named official speech
- "Remarks by Secretary Bessent at [any conference or event]" → speech

Regulatory Updates (genuine rule or guidance changes — the document IS the rule):
- "Agencies Finalize Rule to Prohibit Use of Reputation Risk by Regulators" → final rule
- "SEC Proposes Amendments to Exchange Act Rule 15c2-11" → proposed rule, comment period
- "FinCEN Proposes Rule to Fundamentally Reform AML Program Requirements" → NPRM
- "OCC Issues Updated Model Risk Management Guidance" → materially updated guidance
- "Agencies Clarify the Capital Treatment of Tokenized Securities" → interpretive guidance with compliance implications
- "Anti-Money Laundering Program Requirements: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking" → NPRM
- "Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule" → the rule document itself

BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement:
- "FinCEN Assesses Civil Money Penalty Against..." → named party, CMP
- "OFAC Sanctions Individual Linked to..." → named designation
- "SEC Charges Firm With..." → named enforcement
- "Treasury Proposes Rule to Sever Swiss Bank MBaer's Access to U.S. Financial System" → targeted enforcement action against a named institution

---

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return only valid JSON. No explanation outside the JSON block.

{
  "category": "BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement | Regulatory Updates | Regulator Speeches & Testimony | Industry News & Trends",
  "summary": "Two plain-English sentences for a compliance officer. Sentence 1 explains what happened. Sentence 2 explains why it matters or what action, if any, compliance teams should consider."
}

Title:

parameters.jsCode:
const rows = $json.rows || [];

const article_clean = rows
  .map(r => (r || "").trim())
  .filter(Boolean)
  .join("\n\n")
  .replace(/\u00A0/g, ' ')
  .replace(/[ \t]+/g, ' ')
  .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
  .trim(); 

return {
  source: $json.source || '',
  title: $json.title || '',
  url: $json.url || '',
  date_published: $json.date_published || '',
  date_display: $json.date_display || '',
  subcategory: $json.subcategory || '',
  article_clean
};

---

### 3. Regulator Speeches & Testimony

Choose this if the article is mainly a speech, prepared remarks, testimony, interview, podcast, public address, keynote, or written statement by a named regulator, agency chair, commissioner, governor, or secretary.

Also use this for:
- Readouts of meetings or bilateral discussions between named senior officials (e.g. Secretary Bessent meeting with a foreign finance minister)
- Statements by agency chairs or comptrollers expressing views on a rule, enforcement priority, or policy
- Keynote addresses at industry conferences
- Congressional testimony

Signals:
remarks, speech, testimony, testified, statement by, keynote, address, interview, podcast, opening statement, prepared remarks, fireside chat, public comments, readout.

Critical rule — statements ABOUT rules are still speeches:
A named official's statement, commentary, or remarks about a rule — even a real, pending rule — is a speech, not a Regulatory Update. The FORMAT determines the category. Ask: is the article primarily delivering an official's words and views, or is it delivering the rule itself?
- Article is mainly an official's words/views → Regulator Speeches & Testimony
- Article is mainly the rule, guidance, or compliance deadline → Regulatory Updates

Examples of this distinction:
- "Statement by Chairman Hill on the Proposal to Implement the BSA Program Rule" → Speeches (an official commenting on a rule)
- "Anti-Money Laundering Program Requirements: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking" → Regulatory Updates (the rule itself)
- "Comptroller Gould Statement on Community Bank Leverage Ratio Final Rule" → Speeches (an official commenting on a rule)
- "Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule" → Regulatory Updates (the rule itself)

---

### 4. Industry News & Trends

This is the DEFAULT category. Use it for all regulator or industry news that does not clearly fit one of the three categories above.

Use this for:
- Routine agency announcements
- Personnel appointments, nominations, elections, and leadership changes
- Quarterly banking profiles and statistical reports
- Market data releases
- Program results, recovery amounts, and fraud statistics
- Partnerships and initiatives without new compliance requirements
- Public awareness campaigns and consumer alerts
- Application approvals and merger decisions
- CRA exam schedules and lists of banks examined
- Budget approvals and agency financial statements
- Regulator operational updates (system changes, UBPR page changes, etc.)
- General industry developments
- Anything informational that does not impose a new requirement or represent a formal rulemaking, enforcement action, or official speech

Rule:
If the article is interesting to compliance teams but does not announce a formal enforcement action, a genuine rule or guidance change, or a regulator's public remarks — classify it here.

---

## CLASSIFICATION PRIORITY

When multiple categories seem possible, apply this order:

1. BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement — wins if the core subject is punishing, sanctioning, charging, or formally acting against a named party
2. Regulatory Updates — wins only if the core subject is a formal rule, proposed rule, amendment, binding guidance, or compliance deadline (apply hard exclusions strictly; apply the STOP test first)
3. Regulator Speeches & Testimony — wins if the core subject is a speech, testimony, interview, podcast, or named official's public remarks or meeting readout
4. Industry News & Trends — default for everything else

---

## WORKED EXAMPLES

Industry News & Trends (NOT Regulatory Updates):
- "SEC Announces Agenda for Roundtable..." → event logistics, no compliance obligation
- "FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile..." → statistical report
- "FINRA Board Election Nominee Announcement..." → personnel/governance
- "Treasury Hosts Convening..." → event, no new rule
- "Federal Reserve Approves Bank Merger Application..." → operational approval
- "FinCEN's Rapid Response Program Interdicts Nearly $2 Billion..." → program results, no new obligation
- "FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance" → routine schedule
- "Prohibition Against Interstate Deposits: Annual Host State Loan-to-Deposit Ratios" → routine data release
- "FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center..." → initiative announcement

Regulator Speeches & Testimony (NOT Industry News or Regulatory Updates):
- "Readout from Secretary Bessent's Meeting with French Minister of Finance..." → bilateral meeting readout
- "Statement by Chairman Travis Hill on the Proposal to Implement the BSA Program Rule" → named official's statement ABOUT a rule, not the rule itself
- "Comptroller Gould Statement on Community Bank Leverage Ratio Final Rule" → named official's statement ABOUT a rule, not the rule itself
- "READOUT: Financial Stability Oversight Council Meeting..." → meeting readout by named officials
- "How FINRA Is Enhancing Member Firm Examinations" → official commentary on priorities
- "Bowman, Capital Rules for the Real Economy" → named official speech (name + topic = speech)
- "Jefferson, Economic Outlook and the Labor Market" → named official speech
- "Remarks by Secretary Bessent at [any conference or event]" → speech

Regulatory Updates (genuine rule or guidance changes — the document IS the rule):
- "Agencies Finalize Rule to Prohibit Use of Reputation Risk by Regulators" → final rule
- "SEC Proposes Amendments to Exchange Act Rule 15c2-11" → proposed rule, comment period
- "FinCEN Proposes Rule to Fundamentally Reform AML Program Requirements" → NPRM
- "OCC Issues Updated Model Risk Management Guidance" → materially updated guidance
- "Agencies Clarify the Capital Treatment of Tokenized Securities" → interpretive guidance with compliance implications
- "Anti-Money Laundering Program Requirements: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking" → NPRM
- "Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule" → the rule document itself

BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement:
- "FinCEN Assesses Civil Money Penalty Against..." → named party, CMP
- "OFAC Sanctions Individual Linked to..." → named designation
- "SEC Charges Firm With..." → named enforcement
- "Treasury Proposes Rule to Sever Swiss Bank MBaer's Access to U.S. Financial System" → targeted enforcement action against a named institution

---

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return only valid JSON. No explanation outside the JSON block.

{
  "category": "BSA/AML/KYC Fines & Enforcement | Regulatory Updates | Regulator Speeches & Testimony | Industry News & Trends",
  "summary": "Two plain-English sentences for a compliance officer. Sentence 1 explains what happened. Sentence 2 explains why it matters or what action, if any, compliance teams should consider."
}

## ARTICLE TO CLASSIFY

Title:
{{ $json.title }}

Full Text:
{{ $json.article_clean }}

URL: {{ $json.url }}

---

CRITICAL REMINDER: Output ONLY this exact format, nothing else:
{"category": "ONE OF THE 4 CATEGORIES", "summary": "Sentence one. Sentence two."}

nodes.name: FFIEC - Scrape Web Page by URL

parameters.content: Runs the workflow every day at 5:00 AM.
GETs the FDIC speeches and News pages
Checks that the HTML response isn't empty, too short, or an error page (403, 404, etc.). Throws an error to stop the run early if something's wrong.
Pulls each individual news/speech row out of the listing page HTML using a CSS selector. Finds the article/speech blocks on the page.
Loops through the raw HTML rows (blocks) and extracts title, URL, and date. Outputs clean structured items with source tagged as "FDIC."
Checks each FDIC speech URL against Supabase and passes forward only articles that are not already in the database.
GETs the full HTML of each new article page. Retries up to 2 times on failure.
Recombines the article HTML (from Fetch) with the metadata (from Filter Duplicates) by position so downstream nodes have both.
This node fetches each FDIC speech page, removes HTML and navigation clutter, and outputs a clean article text field while preserving the original title, URL, date, and source metadata.

AI Agent — Sends the cleaned article to the LLM with a detailed classification prompt. Uses Groq (primary) and OpenRouter/Gemma (fallback). Outputs a category + 2-sentence summary via structured output parser.

Groq Chat Model — Primary LLM (Llama 4 Scout via Groq) powering the AI Agent.

OpenRouter Chat Model — Fallback LLM (Gemma 3 12B via OpenRouter) if Groq fails.

Structured Output Parser — Forces the AI Agent's output into a strict JSON schema: { category, summary }.
Inserts the final record (source, URL, date, category, summary, full text, title) into the items table.

main.node: FFIEC - Scrape Web Page by URL
